qualifying_org_definition: Religious or community organizations where the ABAWD performs unpaid or volunteer work; OAC 340:50-5-100 permits 'paid, in-kind, unpaid, or volunteer work with religious or community organizations' to satisfy the 80 hours/month requirement. No pre-approved list, MOU, or county assignment is required for the general volunteer lane.
verification_instrument: Case-note documentation by OKDHS worker using Form 08AD092E (Client Contact and Information Request) to list required verifications; OAC 340:50-3-3 requires the household to 'verify the hours the ABAWD works or participates in a work program' but specifies no volunteer-specific form — client statement is accepted unless questionable, per the fsquest ABAWD training resource and the generic verification standard in OAC 340:50-3-3.
hours_formula_detail: OAC 340:50-5-100 explicitly states 'works continuously 20 hours or more per week, averaged monthly. Averaged monthly means 80 hours per month.' Oklahoma follows the federal baseline directly.
accepts_outofstate_detail: OAC 340:50-5-100 and OAC 340:50-3-3 do not mention any location, in-state registration, or 501(c)(3) status requirement for qualifying volunteer organizations. The regulation refers only to 'religious or community organizations' with no geographic restriction. No OKDHS guidance found addressing this. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit may serve as a volunteer site absent a state restriction — silence is operationally favorable.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Oklahoma had no ABAWD geographic waiver as of Q4 FY2024 and does not appear on any FY2025-2026 waiver list. Oklahoma is not among states historically using geographic waivers.
submission_deadline: Recipient must report when hours drop below 80/month within 10 calendar days of the month the hours dropped (OAC 340:50-5-100). Routine positive verification of hours is provided when requested by the caseworker, typically at application or recertification. No separate monthly hours submission deadline found in policy.
notes:
Oklahoma is Tier 1 clean: OAC 340:50-5-100 explicitly allows unpaid or volunteer work with religious or community organizations to satisfy the ABAWD 80 hours/month requirement, with no mandatory EDP, MOU, county assignment, or provider registration mentioned as a prerequisite. OK SNAP Works is a voluntary E&T program and is not a required gate for self-directed volunteer credit. Confidence is medium because no named state verification form was found — the regulation only states that the ABAWD must 'verify his or her work or participation hours' without specifying a form, and direct OKDHS policy manuals were not fully accessible during research.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial searches conducted to try to refute the Tier 1 classification. Searches targeted: (1) mandatory work registration intake/EDP requirement, (2) OK SNAP Works enrollment as a gate, (3) a state-maintained approved volunteer site list, (4) MOU or contract requirement for nonprofits, (5) the FFY2025 OK SNAP Works State Plan PDF (binary — not extractable), (6) OAC 340:50-3-3 verification rule, (7) the OKDHS Rule Impact Statement 25-08, (8) Hunger Free Oklahoma explainer, (9) OKDHS E&T provider registration page.

Findings: No refuting evidence found. OK SNAP Works is confirmed voluntary in multiple state sources (OKDHS website uses 'voluntary program' language; E&T provider registration page states 'not accepting applications at this time' and is separate from the general volunteer lane). OAC 340:50-5-100(a)(1) lists volunteer work with religious or community organizations as a standalone qualifying activity — it does not require prior enrollment in OK SNAP Works or any formal program. No state-maintained approved-site list was found. No MOU or contract requirement for nonprofits hosting general volunteers was found. Work registration is a caseworker screening/coding step at application, not a participant-facing gate that blocks volunteer hours from counting.

Verification instrument clarified: OAC 340:50-3-3 uses Form 08AD092E (Client Contact and Information Request) to list verifications needed; for volunteer hours the standard is client statement accepted unless questionable, documented in case notes — consistent with self-reported hours. No named volunteer-specific form exists.

Remote stance remains unknown — no Oklahoma source addresses whether remote/online volunteer work counts.

Confidence raised to HIGH: the regulation text is unambiguous on the general volunteer lane, OK SNAP Works is confirmed non-mandatory from the state plan and OKDHS site, and the adversarial pass found no hidden gating step. The only remaining unknown (remote stance) is a product-design question, not a Tier classification question.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: OAC 340:50-5-100 explicitly states '80 hours per month' as the monthly equivalent of 20 hours/week — federal_80 applies, no workfare formula. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: Policy is silent — no location, in-state registration, or 501(c)(3) requirement appears anywhere in OAC 340:50-5-100 or OAC 340:50-3-3; federal default permitting any qualifying nonprofit applies. (3) Geographic waivers: None — Oklahoma not listed on FNA ABAWD waiver pages for FY2025-2029 and confirmed by multiple sources as fully enforcing ABAWD statewide with no county-level waivers as of 2026. (4) Submission mechanism: No ABAWD-specific channel found; general OKDHS reporting uses paper_county_office and online_portal (OKDHSLive); the regulation requires reporting hour drops within 10 calendar days but routine positive verification is caseworker-requested at recertification. (5) Form fields: No named volunteer verification form exists; the default reasonable baseline fields apply.
